name: ai-agent-manager-plugin
description: AI agents with enhanced QA Executor v7.3 (generic signal→pattern architecture, 10 new discovery-driven test patterns, interaction-level coverage tracking, response leak detection, failure classification, Phase 2B header/cookie/credential capture, CSS locator enforcement gate, --scope budget 75, 13-phase protocol: infrastructure discovery, pre-existing test triage, post-generation self-check gate, auth linear chains, boundary test enforcement, email flow testing via Mailpit/MailHog), enhanced QA Strategist (structural completeness audit with 6 checks), enhanced Code Reviewer (LSP diagnostics, effort:high, permissionMode:plan, schema v2 issue categories, REVIEW.md support, Stop hook), parallel orchestration, plan-first workflows, structured result schemas, failure escalation rules, session logging, and .supervisor/ state management. 11 agent roles with YAML frontmatter (color, disallowedTools, per-agent hooks, maxTurns, effort, permissionMode): Launch Pad (Supervisor readiness with codebase analysis and jobs lifecycle), Supervisor v4 (parallel orchestrator with merge safety gate), Execute Manager (Phase 3 poll loop with worker/reviewer lifecycle), Context-Keeper (externalized state with concurrency model), Worker (isolated implementation with schema-validated results), Product Owner (business requirements), Orchestrator (task planning), Code Reviewer (LSP-powered quality gates with issue categorization), Red Team Reviewer (adversarial audits with memory), QA Strategist (risk-based test strategy, structural completeness audit, assertion quality with loose-error-match detection), and QA Executor (13-phase discovery-driven functional tests with self-check gates, infrastructure-aware email testing, auth chain generation, boundary enforcement, pre-existing test triage). Features 44 reusable skills with version tracking, structured result schemas (WORKER_RESULT, EXECUTE_RESULT, QA_RESULT with pre-existing test fields, QA_SESSION, CODE_REVIEW_RESULT v2), failure escalation rules, architecture contracts, 6 quality gate hooks, and persistent agent memory across 6 agents.
